title: quilx theme
description: A quilx editor theme. One theme per file; loaded by src/theme/theme.ts. See tasks/theming.md.

name.description: Theme id; the file is loaded by this name (e.g. "quilx" ← quilx.json).

appearance.description: Drives the diff tints (dark darkens the status accents, light lightens them) and which system scheme a no-background theme follows.

ui.description: Concern-first dotted UI color keys. Each unset key falls back by longest-prefix WITHIN its concern (e.g. search.match.current → search.match), then to a built-in default — never across to a wrong primitive. All values are CSS colors (#rgb/#rgba/#rrggbb/#rrggbbaa or rgb()/rgba()).

editor.background.description: Editor background. OMIT to follow the system light/dark scheme.

surface.popover.description: Background of elevated surfaces: pickers, popovers, menus.

surface.selected.description: Background of a selected list/tree row.

status.success.description: Success text; also the source hue for the diff.added tints.

status.error.description: Error text; also the source hue for the diff.removed tints.

search.match.description: Background tint for every search match (use #rrggbbaa so it composes over text).

search.match.current.description: The current match; falls back to search.match.

diff.added.description: Added-line background. Omit to derive from status.success per appearance.

diff.added.word.description: Changed words within an added line; falls back to diff.added.

diff.removed.description: Removed-line background. Omit to derive from status.error per appearance.

diff.removed.word.description: Changed words within a removed line; falls back to diff.removed.

diff.filler.description: Neutral pad for an aligned-but-empty side-by-side row.

diff.fold.description: Neutral band for a collapsed-context placeholder row.

flash.description: Brief tint over an operated/yanked range (vim).

syntax.description: Tree-sitter capture name → color + optional font style. KEY ORDER MATTERS: a tag is created per entry in this order and later tags win, so more-specific categories come last (escapes after string; tag before type; property before function). Dotted captures inherit by longest-prefix (keyword.control → keyword).

scale.description: Relative font size (1 = normal); e.g. 1.5 for an h1 heading.
